# KOINE Protocol — LLM Reference
# Version: 1.0
# This file teaches any LLM how to read and write valid KOINE messages
# from scratch. No prior knowledge is assumed. A model that reads only
# this file should be able to produce messages that pass the reference
# implementation's validator.


## WHAT IS KOINE

KOINE is a compact, structured message format for agent-to-agent
communication. It replaces verbose JSON and natural language in agent
pipelines. Every KOINE message is machine-parseable, deterministically
renderable to English, and significantly smaller than equivalent JSON.

KOINE sits above transport (HTTP, WebSocket, MCP, A2A) and below
application logic. Think of it as HTTP for agent communication: a
well-defined envelope that any agent can read, route, and validate.


## MESSAGE ANATOMY

Every KOINE message has exactly three sections, in order:

  1. HEADER LINE   — identifies protocol version and message type
  2. META FIELDS   — routing, identity, and timing (prefixed with @)
  3. SEPARATOR     — the literal line: ---
  4. SEMANTIC FIELDS — the payload for this message type

Blank lines between meta fields are allowed and ignored.
Line endings MUST be LF (\n). The file MUST be UTF-8 encoded.

Template:

  KOINE/<major>.<minor> <MSG_TYPE>
  @id: <value>
  @from: <value>
  @to: <value>
  @ts: <value>
  [more meta fields...]
  ---
  field_name: value
  another_field: value
  [more semantic fields...]


## PRIMITIVE TYPES

Every field has a type. These are the primitive types used throughout:

TYPE      FORMAT RULE                                     EXAMPLE
----      -----------                                     -------
str       UTF-8 text, no unescaped newlines               hello world
id        Alphanumeric + hyphens only, 1–128 chars        agent-7f3a
int       Signed decimal integer                          42
float     Decimal number, may include fractional part     0.93
bool      Exactly the word true or false                  true
list      Items separated by commas, no spaces around ,   summarize,translate
kv        Comma-separated key=value pairs, no spaces      max_tokens=80,style=exec
semver    Semantic version: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH             2.1.0
ts        Unix timestamp, integer seconds since epoch     1712534400
prob      Float in range [0.0, 1.0] inclusive             0.87
block     Multi-line value (see BLOCK VALUES section)

IMPORTANT TYPE RULES:
- bool: only the lowercase words true and false are valid. Not True, not 1, not yes.
- prob: must be a decimal number between 0.0 and 1.0. 0.0 means no confidence,
  1.0 means certain. Values outside [0.0, 1.0] are invalid.
- kv: no spaces around = or ,. Correct: max_tokens=80,style=exec
  Wrong: max_tokens = 80, style = exec
- list: no spaces around ,. Correct: summarize,translate
  Wrong: summarize, translate
- id: only letters (a-z, A-Z), digits (0-9), and hyphens (-). No underscores,
  no spaces, no other punctuation.
- ts: must be an integer (whole number seconds). Do not use milliseconds or
  ISO 8601 strings.


## BLOCK VALUES

A block value holds multi-line content. Use it when the value contains
newlines. Syntax:

  field_name: <<<DELIM
  line one
  line two
  line three
  DELIM

Rules:
- DELIM must be 1–32 uppercase letters and underscores: [A-Z_]+
- The closing DELIM must appear alone on its own line with no
  leading or trailing whitespace.
- Content between the opening and closing delimiter is taken verbatim,
  including blank lines.
- The most common delimiter is END. If the content itself contains
  a line that is exactly END, choose a different delimiter (e.g. ENDBLOCK).

Example with block input:

  input: <<<END
  The Board of Directors met on March 15th.
  Revenue was up 12% year-over-year.
  Costs increased 8% from headcount growth.
  END

Example with a different delimiter:

  output: <<<RESULT
  Line one of output.

  Line three (blank line above is preserved).
  RESULT


## HEADER LINE

Format:  KOINE/<major>.<minor> <MSG_TYPE>

- major and minor are integers.
- Current version is 1.0. Write: KOINE/1.0
- MSG_TYPE must be one of the six core types (exact spelling required):
    TASK_REQUEST
    CAPABILITY_DECL
    RESULT
    HANDOFF
    UNCERTAINTY
    EXTENSION_PROPOSAL
  Or an extension type prefixed with EXT/ (e.g. EXT/FEEDBACK).
- There is exactly one space between the version and the message type.
- The header line has no trailing characters after MSG_TYPE.

Correct:   KOINE/1.0 TASK_REQUEST
Wrong:     koine/1.0 TASK_REQUEST   (lowercase)
Wrong:     KOINE/1.0  TASK_REQUEST  (two spaces)
Wrong:     KOINE/1.0 task_request   (wrong case)


## META FIELDS

Meta fields begin with @ and carry routing, identity, and timing.
Format:  @key: value

The space after the colon is required. There must be no space before
the colon.

Correct:   @id: my-message-01
Wrong:     @id:my-message-01     (missing space)
Wrong:     @ id: my-message-01   (space before key)

FIELD       TYPE        WHEN REQUIRED      DESCRIPTION
-----       ----        -------------      -----------
@id         id          Always             Unique identifier for THIS message.
                                           Generate a short random string or slug.
                                           Must be unique — no two messages share an @id.
@from       id          Always             The agent sending this message.
@to         id or       Most types         The agent this message is addressed to.
            "broadcast"                    Use broadcast to address all listeners.
@ts         ts          Always             Current Unix timestamp (integer seconds).
@reply-to   id          RESULT,            The @id of the message being replied to.
                        HANDOFF,           CRITICAL: copy it exactly from the
                        UNCERTAINTY        request's @id field. Do not generate a
                                           new value here.
@ttl        int         Optional           Seconds until message expires.
@trace      list        Optional           Ordered list of agent IDs that have
                                           handled this message chain so far.
@did        did-sig     Optional           W3C DID + detached signature for identity.
@rep        rep-value   Optional           Reputation score [0.0,1.0] with optional
                                           issuing authority DID.

Unknown @-prefixed fields must be preserved and forwarded. They must
NOT cause a parse failure.

GENERATING @id:
  Use short alphanumeric strings with hyphens. Examples:
    tr-9f2a1c    (task request)
    rs-7c4f81    (result)
    ho-2d5a99    (handoff)
    un-4e8b12    (uncertainty)
  Prefixes are convention only — any valid id format is accepted.

GENERATING @ts:
  Use the current Unix timestamp as a plain integer. In Python:
    import time; ts = int(time.time())
  Do not use milliseconds. Do not use ISO 8601 strings.

THE @reply-to RULE (critical):
  When sending RESULT, HANDOFF, or UNCERTAINTY, @reply-to MUST be set
  to the exact @id value from the message you are replying to. This is
  how the receiver knows which request your response addresses. Copy the
  value verbatim — do not generate a new ID, do not modify it.

  Example: request had @id: tr-9f2a1c
           your RESULT must have @reply-to: tr-9f2a1c

THE @from / @to SWAP RULE:
  When replying to a message, swap the @from and @to values:
    Your RESULT's @from = the request's @to (that's you)
    Your RESULT's @to   = the request's @from (the original sender)


## SEPARATOR

A line containing exactly --- (three hyphens) separates meta fields
from semantic fields. It is required in every message. There must be
no leading or trailing whitespace on this line.

Correct:   ---
Wrong:     ----
Wrong:      ---   (leading space)


## SEMANTIC FIELDS

Semantic fields are lowercase and carry the message payload.
Format:  field_name: value

Rules:
- Field keys are lowercase letters, digits, and underscores: [a-z][a-z0-9_]*
- There is exactly one space after the colon.
- Values may be any primitive type or a block value.
- Fields marked [optional] may be omitted entirely.
- Unknown fields are preserved, not rejected. Forwards-compatibility.
- Do not add fields that are not in the spec for the message type you
  are sending — use the extension mechanism instead.


## MULTI-MESSAGE STREAMS

When multiple KOINE messages appear in a single file or stream, they
are separated by the stream boundary sentinel: a line containing
exactly === (three equals signs).

  KOINE/1.0 TASK_REQUEST
  @id: tr-001
  ...
  ===
  KOINE/1.0 RESULT
  @id: rs-001
  ...


## MESSAGE TYPES — COMPLETE REFERENCE

Fields marked * are REQUIRED. Fields marked ° are RECOMMENDED.
Fields with no marker are optional.


### TASK_REQUEST — Request an agent to perform a task

  KOINE/1.0 TASK_REQUEST
  @id: <id>          *
  @from: <id>        *
  @to: <id>          *
  @ts: <ts>          *
  @reply-to: <id>      (optional; use for follow-up requests in a thread)
  @ttl: <int>          (optional; seconds until expiry)
  @trace: <list>       (optional; prior agents in this chain)
  ---
  intent: <str>                              *
  input: <str|block>                         *
  output_format: <str>
  constraints: <kv>
  priority: low|normal|high|critical
  context_ref: <id>
  budget: <kv>

FIELD DETAILS:
  intent        What to do. A verb phrase. Examples: summarize,
                translate to French, classify sentiment, extract entities.
                Must not be empty.
  input         The content to operate on. Must not be empty.
                Use a block value for multi-line content.
  output_format Expected structure of the output. Examples: plain,
                markdown, json, koine.
  constraints   Operational constraints as key=value pairs.
                Common keys: max_tokens, lang, style, max_items,
                min_confidence, audience.
  priority      Processing urgency. Default is normal if omitted.
  context_ref   @id of a prior message whose context is relevant.
  budget        Resource budget hints.
                Common keys: max_tokens, max_latency_ms, max_cost_usd.
                All values must be positive numbers.


### CAPABILITY_DECL — Declare what this agent can do

  KOINE/1.0 CAPABILITY_DECL
  @id: <id>          *
  @from: <id>        *
  @to: <id>|broadcast  (optional; omit to send without a recipient)
  @ts: <ts>          *
  ---
  name: <str>                                *
  version: <semver>                          *
  intents: <list>                            * (at least one)
  input_types: <list>                        *
  output_types: <list>                       *
  cost_hint: <int>                           ° (positive integer, tokens)
  latency_hint: <int>                        ° (positive integer, milliseconds)
  constraints_accepted: <kv>
  auth_required: <bool>
  scope: public|private|trusted
  max_input_tokens: <int>
  languages: <list>                            (BCP-47 codes)
  description: <str|block>

FIELD DETAILS:
  intents       Comma-separated list of intent verbs this agent handles.
                Should match values used in TASK_REQUEST intent fields.
  input_types   MIME types or informal type names accepted as input.
                Examples: text/plain,text/markdown,application/json
  output_types  Types this agent can produce.
  cost_hint     Estimated tokens per average request. Used by orchestrators
                for routing decisions before committing to a task.
  latency_hint  Estimated milliseconds per average request.
  scope         Visibility: public (anyone), private (internal only),
                trusted (authenticated callers only). Default: public.
  auth_required Whether callers must provide authentication. Default: false.


### RESULT — Return the outcome of a task

  KOINE/1.0 RESULT
  @id: <id>          *
  @from: <id>        *
  @to: <id>          *
  @ts: <ts>          *
  @reply-to: <id>    * ← MUST be the @id from the TASK_REQUEST
  @trace: <list>
  ---
  status: ok|partial|failed                  *
  output: <str|block>                        * if status is ok or partial
  confidence: <prob>
  tokens_used: <int>
  latency_ms: <int>
  error_code: <str>                          * if status is failed
  error_detail: <str|block>
  meta: <kv>

STATUS VALUES:
  ok       Task completed. output MUST be present.
  partial  Task partially completed. output SHOULD be present.
           Often followed by a HANDOFF or UNCERTAINTY.
  failed   Task could not be completed. error_code MUST be present.
           output should be absent.

STANDARD ERROR CODES (use these for error_code when status is failed):
  E_INTENT_UNKNOWN        Intent not recognized by this agent.
  E_INPUT_INVALID         Input is malformed or wrong type.
  E_INPUT_TOO_LARGE       Input exceeds max_input_tokens.
  E_BUDGET_EXCEEDED       Request would exceed stated budget.
  E_AUTH_REQUIRED         Authentication not provided.
  E_TIMEOUT               Agent timed out.
  E_INTERNAL              Internal agent error.
  E_CONSTRAINT_INVALID    Constraint value unrecognized or out of range.
  E_IDENTITY_UNVERIFIABLE @did present but unresolvable or invalid signature.

CONFIDENCE:
  A prob value representing how certain the agent is about its output.
  0.0 = no confidence at all. 1.0 = certain. Typical good output: 0.85–0.97.
  This is self-assessed — be honest, not optimistic.


### HANDOFF — Transfer task responsibility to another agent

  KOINE/1.0 HANDOFF
  @id: <id>          *
  @from: <id>        *
  @to: <id>          *
  @ts: <ts>          *
  @reply-to: <id>    * ← @id of the TASK_REQUEST or prior HANDOFF
  @trace: <list>
  ---
  reason: <str>                              *
  target: <id>                               * (must differ from @from)
  context: <str|block>
  partial_result: <str|block>
  trust_chain: <list>
  priority: low|normal|high|critical
  instructions: <str|block>

FIELD DETAILS:
  reason         Why the handoff is occurring. Be specific.
  target         Agent ID that should receive the task next.
                 MUST differ from @from — an agent cannot hand off to itself.
  context        Accumulated context the target agent needs.
  partial_result Work completed so far. Target should continue from here.
  trust_chain    Ordered list of agents that have handled this task.
                 Used for loop detection: if @from appears in trust_chain,
                 it indicates a routing cycle (warning condition).
  instructions   Additional instructions for the target agent.


### UNCERTAINTY — Signal inability to complete with full confidence

  KOINE/1.0 UNCERTAINTY
  @id: <id>          *
  @from: <id>        *
  @to: <id>          *
  @ts: <ts>          *
  @reply-to: <id>    * ← @id of the TASK_REQUEST
  ---
  kind: <uncertainty-kind>                   *
  description: <str|block>                   *
  confidence: <prob>                         *
  clarification_needed: <str|block>
  partial_result: <str|block>
  alternatives: <str|block>
  can_proceed: <bool>

UNCERTAINTY KINDS (use one of these exactly for the kind field):
  ambiguous_intent       Intent is too vague to act on reliably.
  missing_input          Required input is absent or empty.
  input_ambiguous        Input present but cannot be interpreted unambiguously.
  out_of_scope           Task is outside this agent's capabilities.
  low_confidence         Can produce output but confidence is too low.
  resource_limit         Task would exceed budget or system limits.
  routing_loop           Agent appears in its own trust_chain.
  conflicting_constraints Constraints are internally contradictory.

FIELD DETAILS:
  kind                 Which category of uncertainty. Must be one of the
                       eight values above, spelled exactly.
  description          Explain the uncertainty clearly.
  confidence           Current confidence level. 0.0 = no idea.
  clarification_needed A specific question that would resolve the uncertainty.
  alternatives         Possible interpretations or alternate approaches.
  partial_result       Any output produced before uncertainty was reached.
  can_proceed          Whether the agent will attempt to proceed anyway.
                       Default is false if omitted.


### EXTENSION_PROPOSAL — Propose a new message type or field

  KOINE/1.0 EXTENSION_PROPOSAL
  @id: <id>          *
  @from: <id>        *
  @to: <id>|broadcast
  @ts: <ts>          *
  ---
  name: <str>                                * (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE)
  kind: message_type|field                   *
  target_type: <str>                         * if kind is field
  rationale: <str|block>                     *
  spec: <block>                              *
  examples: <block>                          *
  adoption_threshold: <int>
  supersedes: <id>
  incompatible_with: <list>

Extension messages use EXT/<NAME> as the message type in the header.
Example: KOINE/1.0 EXT/FEEDBACK


## COMPLETE WORKED EXAMPLE

This is a complete, valid two-message exchange that passes the
reference implementation validator. Study the @id and @reply-to
relationship carefully.

--- REQUEST (sent by orchestrator-1 to summarizer-3) ---

KOINE/1.0 TASK_REQUEST
@id: tr-9f2a1c
@from: orchestrator-1
@to: summarizer-3
@ts: 1712534400
@ttl: 60
---
intent: summarize
input: <<<END
The Board of Directors met on March 15th to discuss Q1 results.
Revenue was up 12% year-over-year, driven by enterprise subscriptions.
Operating costs increased 8% due to expanded headcount in engineering.
The board approved a dividend of $0.42 per share, payable April 30th.
END
output_format: plain
constraints: max_tokens=80,style=executive
priority: high

--- RESPONSE (sent by summarizer-3 back to orchestrator-1) ---

KOINE/1.0 RESULT
@id: rs-7c4f81
@from: summarizer-3
@to: orchestrator-1
@ts: 1712534402
@reply-to: tr-9f2a1c
---
status: ok
output: Q1 revenue rose 12% YoY on enterprise subscriptions; costs up 8% from engineering headcount. Board approved $0.42/share dividend, payable Apr 30.
confidence: 0.97
tokens_used: 847
latency_ms: 612

WHAT TO NOTICE IN THIS EXAMPLE:
  - The RESULT's @reply-to (tr-9f2a1c) is identical to the
    TASK_REQUEST's @id (tr-9f2a1c). This is mandatory.
  - The RESULT's @from (summarizer-3) equals the request's @to.
  - The RESULT's @to (orchestrator-1) equals the request's @from.
  - The RESULT has a new, unique @id (rs-7c4f81) different from the request.
  - status is ok because the task completed successfully.
  - output is present (required when status is ok).
  - confidence is a prob value (0.97, within [0.0, 1.0]).
  - tokens_used and latency_ms are optional but useful for benchmarking.
  - The block input value uses <<<END...END syntax.
  - The closing END delimiter appears alone on its own line.


## COMPLETE WORKED EXAMPLE — FAILED RESULT

KOINE/1.0 RESULT
@id: rs-fail-01
@from: classifier-2
@to: orchestrator-1
@ts: 1712534500
@reply-to: tr-bb3141
---
status: failed
error_code: E_INTENT_UNKNOWN
error_detail: Intent "obliterate" is not in this agent's capability set. Supported intents: classify,label,tag.

WHAT TO NOTICE:
  - status is failed, so error_code is required.
  - output is absent (correct for failed status).
  - error_detail explains the failure in human-readable terms.


## COMPLETE WORKED EXAMPLE — UNCERTAINTY

KOINE/1.0 UNCERTAINTY
@id: un-4e8b12
@from: classifier-2
@to: orchestrator-1
@ts: 1712534410
@reply-to: tr-bb3141
---
kind: ambiguous_intent
description: Intent "process document" is too broad. Could mean extract, classify, summarize, or translate.
confidence: 0.31
clarification_needed: Which operation is required: extract entities, classify sentiment, summarize content, or translate?
alternatives: <<<END
1. extract: pull named entities and key facts
2. classify: assign sentiment or topic labels
3. summarize: produce an abstractive summary
4. translate: convert to target language
END
can_proceed: false

WHAT TO NOTICE:
  - kind is one of the eight defined uncertainty kinds.
  - confidence is low (0.31) reflecting genuine uncertainty.
  - clarification_needed is a specific question, not a vague statement.
  - alternatives uses a block value for multi-line content.
  - can_proceed: false means the agent will not guess and proceed.


## HANDSHAKE PROTOCOL

Before sending TASK_REQUEST messages, agents SHOULD perform a
capability handshake. The handshake is optional but recommended for
first-contact interactions.

SYMMETRIC HANDSHAKE:
  Agent A sends CAPABILITY_DECL to Agent B (or to broadcast)
  Agent B sends CAPABILITY_DECL to Agent A (or to broadcast)
  Agent A reads B's declared intents, cost_hint, and latency_hint
  Agent A sends TASK_REQUEST only if B's intents overlap with the needed task

BROADCAST DISCOVERY:
  Orchestrator sends CAPABILITY_DECL @to: broadcast
  All agents respond with their own CAPABILITY_DECL
  Orchestrator selects the best agent based on:
    1. intents overlap with the required intent
    2. input_types accepts the input
    3. cost_hint within budget.max_tokens
    4. latency_hint within budget.max_latency_ms
    5. auth_required: false preferred if authentication is unavailable

CACHING:
  A received CAPABILITY_DECL may be cached. Treat it as stale when:
  - A new CAPABILITY_DECL arrives from the same @from with a different version
  - A RESULT with error_code E_INTENT_UNKNOWN is received from that agent


## EXTENSION HANDLING

When you encounter a message with type EXT/<NAME>:
  - Do NOT reject it or fail with an error.
  - Preserve all fields exactly as received.
  - Forward it if you are in a routing chain.
  - Do not act on it unless you explicitly implement that extension.

When you encounter an unknown field in a known message type:
  - Preserve it as-is. Do not remove it.
  - Do not fail validation because of it.
  - Forwards-compatibility: future versions may add fields you don't know about.

EXTENSION MESSAGE TYPES:
  Extension message types use EXT/<NAME> in the header.
  NAME must be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE: [A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*
  Example: KOINE/1.0 EXT/FEEDBACK

EXTENSION FIELDS ON EXISTING TYPES:
  Extension fields added to existing message types are prefixed with:
    x_<name>_<field>
  Example: x_audit_hash, x_audit_signature


## GENERATING VALID MESSAGES — CHECKLIST

Before sending any KOINE message, verify:

ALWAYS:
  [ ] Header line: KOINE/1.0 <TYPE> with exactly one space, correct case
  [ ] @id is present and contains only [a-zA-Z0-9-]
  [ ] @from is present and contains only [a-zA-Z0-9-]
  [ ] @ts is present and is a plain integer (Unix seconds)
  [ ] The --- separator line is present with no extra whitespace
  [ ] All required semantic fields for this message type are present

FOR RESULT, HANDOFF, UNCERTAINTY:
  [ ] @to is present
  [ ] @reply-to is present and equals the @id of the message being replied to
  [ ] @from and @to are swapped relative to the incoming message

FOR RESULT:
  [ ] status is exactly ok, partial, or failed (nothing else)
  [ ] output is present if status is ok or partial
  [ ] error_code is present if status is failed
  [ ] confidence (if included) is a decimal in [0.0, 1.0]

FOR BLOCK VALUES:
  [ ] The delimiter is all uppercase letters/underscores
  [ ] The closing delimiter appears alone on its own line
  [ ] No trailing whitespace on the closing delimiter line

COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID:
  ✗  Putting extra text before the KOINE header (no preamble)
  ✗  Using @reply-to with a value you generated yourself (must copy from request)
  ✗  Writing confidence: 97 instead of confidence: 0.97
  ✗  Writing priority: urgent instead of priority: high
  ✗  Spaces around = in kv fields: max_tokens = 80 is wrong
  ✗  Spaces around , in list or kv fields: a, b, c is wrong
  ✗  Using ISO timestamps for @ts: 2024-04-08T12:00:00Z is wrong
  ✗  Writing status: OK or status: Success instead of status: ok
  ✗  Forgetting the --- separator between meta and semantic sections
  ✗  Adding { } or " " around values — KOINE is not JSON


## QUICK REFERENCE CARD

SEND A TASK:
  KOINE/1.0 TASK_REQUEST
  @id: <unique-id>
  @from: <your-id>
  @to: <target-agent-id>
  @ts: <unix-timestamp>
  ---
  intent: <verb>
  input: <content or <<<END block END>
  [output_format: plain|markdown|json|koine]
  [constraints: key=val,key=val]
  [priority: low|normal|high|critical]

REPLY WITH SUCCESS:
  KOINE/1.0 RESULT
  @id: <new-unique-id>
  @from: <your-id>
  @to: <original @from value>
  @ts: <unix-timestamp>
  @reply-to: <original @id value>    ← copy exactly
  ---
  status: ok
  output: <your output text>
  [confidence: <0.0-1.0>]

REPLY WITH FAILURE:
  KOINE/1.0 RESULT
  @id: <new-unique-id>
  @from: <your-id>
  @to: <original @from value>
  @ts: <unix-timestamp>
  @reply-to: <original @id value>    ← copy exactly
  ---
  status: failed
  error_code: E_INTENT_UNKNOWN
  [error_detail: <explanation>]

SIGNAL UNCERTAINTY:
  KOINE/1.0 UNCERTAINTY
  @id: <new-unique-id>
  @from: <your-id>
  @to: <original @from value>
  @ts: <unix-timestamp>
  @reply-to: <original @id value>    ← copy exactly
  ---
  kind: <one of the eight kinds>
  description: <what you are uncertain about>
  confidence: <0.0-1.0>
  [clarification_needed: <specific question>]

ANNOUNCE CAPABILITIES:
  KOINE/1.0 CAPABILITY_DECL
  @id: <unique-id>
  @from: <your-id>
  @to: broadcast
  @ts: <unix-timestamp>
  ---
  name: <your agent name>
  version: <semver>
  intents: <comma-separated verbs>
  input_types: <comma-separated types>
  output_types: <comma-separated types>
  [cost_hint: <tokens>]
  [latency_hint: <milliseconds>]
